scheme_name: Compensation for Livestock Loss Caused by Wild Animals
page_title: Compensation for Livestock Loss Caused by Wild Animals

details: The “Compensation for Livestock Loss Caused by Wild Animals” scheme is launched by the Forest Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to provide financial relief to individuals whose livestock is injured or killed by wild animals. The scheme applies to both urban and rural areas and covers a variety of livestock including cows, buffaloes, camels, goats, sheep, donkeys, mules, and pigs.
benefits: 50% of the amount payable for injury under revenue circular provisions.
Compensation is paid directly to the beneficiary via e-payment.
Compensation is provided within 30 working days of claim verification.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
The applicant should be the owner of the livestock injured or killed by wild animals.

offline: Madhya Pradesh Lok Sewa Guarantee Portal
On the homepage, click "Citizen Registration". You will be taken to the registration page.
On the registration page, fill in all the mandatory fields (name, mobile number, gender, and captcha) and click "Verify Mobile Number."
A verification code will be sent to your mobile number. Enter the code and complete the registration.
Visit the Official Website of "Madhya Pradesh Lok Sewa Guarantee Portal".
Enter your User ID, Password, and Captcha Code, and click "Login."
Click on "Apply Online Service" and select the scheme you intend to apply for.

In the scheme's online application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size (fields marked with * are mandatory), and click "Submit".
The incident must be reported within 48 hours to the nearest Forest Range Officer, either verbally or in writing.
The dead or injured animal should remain at the site for verification by the forest authority.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply?
faqs.answer: Any livestock owner in Madhya Pradesh whose animals are killed or injured by wild animals.

faqs.question: Which animals are covered?
faqs.answer: Cow, buffalo, camel, bull, horse, sheep, goat, donkey, mule, and pig.

faqs.question: What is the compensation for injury to livestock?
faqs.answer: 50% of the applicable compensation amount under revenue circular provisions.

faqs.question: What is the application fee?
faqs.answer: The application is free of cost.

faqs.question: Where should the application be submitted?
faqs.answer: At the nearest Forest Range Office or online at mpedistrict.gov.in

faqs.question: Within what time should the incident be reported?
faqs.answer: The incident be reported within 48 hours of occurrence.

faqs.question: What is the expected time for payment?
faqs.answer: Payment within 30 working days of verification.

faqs.question: How is the compensation disbursed?
faqs.answer: Directly to the beneficiary via e-payment.

faqs.question: What if my application is rejected?
faqs.answer: A first appeal can be filed within 30 days, and a second appeal within 60 days.

faqs.question: Are urban livestock owners eligible?
faqs.answer: Yes, both urban and rural livestock owners are eligible.

faqs.question: Can I move the dead animal before verification?
faqs.answer: No, the dead animal must remain at the site for verification by the Forest Officer.
